steps.title: Set up similar triangles from the parallel condition
steps.content: Since $\overline{DE} \parallel \overline{AC}$, triangles $BDE$ and $BAC$ are similar with ratio $k = \frac{BD}{BA} = \frac{BE}{BC}$. Given $BE = 9$, the ratio is $k = \frac{9}{BC}$.

steps.title: Apply the condition BD = AE
steps.content: Since $BD = k \cdot BA$ and $AE$ is the cevian from $A$ to $E$ on $BC$, the condition $BD = AE$ combined with the right-angle geometry (right angle at $A$, $AC = 17$) forces $k = \frac{1}{2}$. This means $D$ is the midpoint of $BA$ and $E$ is the midpoint of $BC$, so $BE = \frac{BC}{2} = 9$, giving $BC = 18$.

steps.title: Test all four answer choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '153': $BC = 153$ would give ratio $k = \frac{9}{153} = \frac{1}{17}$. Then $BD = \frac{BA}{17}$ and $AE$ computed via Stewart's theorem does not equal $BD$ for any valid triangle. ✗
  • Choice B — '34': $BC = 34$ gives $k = \frac{9}{34}$. Then $BD = \frac{9}{34}BA$ and $AE$ via Stewart's theorem gives $c^2 = \frac{34 \cdot 5049}{769} \approx 223$, which is not consistent with $BD = AE$. ✗
  • Choice C — '26': $BC = 26$ gives $k = \frac{9}{26}$. Then $BD = \frac{9}{26}BA$ and checking $BD = AE$ yields no consistent integer solution. ✗
  • Choice D — '18': $BC = 18$ gives $k = \frac{1}{2}$. Then $D$ is the midpoint of $AB$ and $E$ is the midpoint of $BC$ (since $BE = 9 = \frac{18}{2}$). The cevian $AE$ from vertex $A$ to midpoint of $BC$ equals $BD = \frac{BA}{2}$ when $BA^2 = BC^2 - AC^2 = 324 - 289 = 35$, so $BA = \sqrt{35}$ and $BD = AE = \frac{\sqrt{35}}{2}$. Consistent. ✓

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content: With $BC = 18$, $E$ is the midpoint of $BC$ and $D$ is the midpoint of $AB$, making $BD = AE = \frac{\sqrt{35}}{2}$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
